title: Switch to race-held-out, chronology-aware validation and ordered categorical training
summary: Repeated laps from the same race create strong within-race dependence, and high-cardinality categories like Driver/Race/Year are leakage-prone under naive row CV.
rationale: Repeated laps from the same race create strong within-race dependence, and high-cardinality categories like Driver/Race/Year are leakage-prone under naive row CV. CatBoost explicitly supports preserving row order for ordered target statistics.
implementation_hint: Sort rows by Year, Race, LapNumber, then compare `LeaveOneGroupOut` on `Race_Year` or forward-in-time year splits against current CV. If using CatBoost, test `has_time=True` and timestamps so CTRs are computed in sequence rather than from shuffled permutations.
expected_effect: More trustworthy local CV and often better public-LB alignment; may also improve generalization on unseen race conditions.
risk: Local CV may drop at first because the split is harder; rare races/drivers may become noisier.
